summary:
The patient’s erectile dysfunction combined with bilateral leg claudication and absent femoral pulses strongly suggests aortoiliac atherosclerosis due to reduced arterial perfusion from severe peripheral vasculopathy. This condition explains both systemic ischemia affecting pelvic arteries (causing ED) and distal limb vessels (leading to exercise-induced pain). 

$
